title: Bridge Essay: EEG Brain Rhythms During Resting-State Wakefulness and Sleep in Elderly Expert M

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Formless/Absorption** (Phase 8) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Formless/Absorption to mechanistic neuroscience.

**EEG Brain Rhythms During Resting-State Wakefulness and Sleep in Elderly Expert Meditators.** — J Sleep Res. PMID: 40731281. DOI: 10.1111/jsr.70161. Meditation practice has been shown to impact resting-state EEG activity in expert meditators, but its benefits on sleep, which is particularly affected with age, are poorly understood. Our aim was to better understand the effects of long-term meditation practice on resting-state EEG and sleep in older adults. Twenty-seven elderly expert meditators (mean age ± SD: 70.7 ± 5.0 years) were compared to meditation-naive controls (69.3 ± 3.8 years) for sleep questionnaires (n = 135), polysomnography (n
